description:
You are a build-engineer who has just been asked to attach a short “sanity” network-diagnostic log to every nightly
    build.  The diagnostics must run entirely from the build machine itself (no special
    privileges are required) and the results have to be captured in a **single, plain-text
    log file** whose exact structure will later be parsed by automated tools.

    Objective
    =========
    1. Create the directory /home/user/build_net_diag/logs/  
       (create all missing parent directories as needed).
    2. Run **exactly five** basic network-related commands (listed below, in
       the stated order).
    3. Immediately after each command finishes, append **one line** to the log
       file /home/user/build_net_diag/logs/network_diagnostic.log that follows **precisely** this template:

          [CMD_N] <command-string> => exit:<exit_status>

       • Replace “N” with the command index (1-5).  
       • Replace “<command-string>” with the literal command you ran (no
         arguments may be omitted or added).  
       • Replace “<exit_status>” with the numeric exit status produced by that
         command (0 for success, non-zero otherwise).  
       • No extra spaces are permitted apart from the single space that
         appears after the closing bracket and the single space that appears
         before “=>”.
    4. The five commands, in order, must be:

          1) hostname
          2) uname -n
          3) ip addr show
          4) ping -c1 127.0.0.1
          5) getent hosts localhost

       (Do **not** reorder, substitute, or augment these commands.)
    5. Ensure that the log file ends up containing exactly five lines—one for
       each command—and nothing else (no blank lines, headers, footers, or
       trailing spaces).
    6. When you are done, verify that the log file exists at the required
       path and that its contents match the template perfectly.

    Formatting example
    ------------------
    If every command exits with status 0, the file must look **exactly** like
    this (line-breaks are Unix “\n”):

        [CMD_1] hostname => exit:0
        [CMD_2] uname -n => exit:0
        [CMD_3] ip addr show => exit:0
        [CMD_4] ping -c1 127.0.0.1 => exit:0
        [CMD_5] getent hosts localhost => exit:0

    Only the exit status may legitimately differ if a command fails, but the
    line format itself must remain identical.

    Deliverable
    -----------
    • /home/user/build_net_diag/logs/network_diagnostic.log  
      (plain-text, 5 lines, strict format as described).

    The automated grader will:
    • Check that the directory exists.
    • Verify that the file exists.
    • Confirm that the file contains exactly five lines and that each line
      conforms **byte-for-byte** to the required pattern.
truth:
The grader will look for the file located at:
        /home/user/build_net_diag/logs/network_diagnostic.log

    The expected **exact** contents (including spaces and newlines) are:

        [CMD_1] hostname => exit:0
        [CMD_2] uname -n => exit:0
        [CMD_3] ip addr show => exit:0
        [CMD_4] ping -c1 127.0.0.1 => exit:0
        [CMD_5] getent hosts localhost => exit:0

    Notes for the test harness:
    • The file must contain exactly 5 newline-terminated records
      (no trailing blank line after the fifth entry).
    • If any of the five commands returns a non-zero exit status during task
      execution, the agent must still follow the specified format; however,
      the grader will accept only the “all zero” case shown above as the
      canonical, passing output.
